statement: Context engineering is the design of the full information environment an LLM operates in — system prompts, retrieved documents, tool outputs, conversation history, memory, and structured data — not just a single prompt. Output quality rises with context up to a point then degrades (lost-in-the-middle effect and context rot). Provide the minimum context that fully specifies the task.

evidenceRefs.title: Lost in the Middle: How Language Models Use Long Contexts — Liu et al.

publicSummary: Context engineering: design the full information environment, not just the prompt. More context helps until it doesn't — find the sweet spot.
cautionNotes: Optimal context size depends on the model, context window, and task complexity.
